name: Moroccan Arabic (Darija)

ancestors.notes: Berber (Tamazight/Tachelhit) substrate: CHIEF cause of vowel deletion; emphatic system reinforced; consonant cluster tolerance from Berber phonotactics

notes: Moroccan Arabic 'Darija' (دارجة). Most divergent mainstream dialect. DEFINING FEATURE: extreme short-vowel reduction — Noamane (2020) p.49: the vocalic inventory is 'limited to three underlying short vowels /i, u, a/ and an epenthetic schwa /ə/' with no long vowels; Heath (2020) p.216: the koiné 'reduce[s] all three short vowels to just one short vowel ə', original short vowels 'syncopate in weak metrical positions', yielding word-initial consonant clusters (Watson 2002 p.13, Moroccan smin 'fat' vs Cairene simīn). ج jim → /ʒ/ (voiced postalveolar fricative, NOT the affricate /dʒ/): Watson (2002) p.16 'in the majority of Maghribi dialects (cf. Heath 1987:20–1 for Moroccan), the phoneme does not have an initial occlusive element and is realized as /ž/'; Ennaji et al. (2004) p.2 inventory lists /ž/ and no /dʒ/. Interdentals merged to stops: ث θ → /t/, ذ ð → /d/, ظ/ض → /dˤ/ — Watson (2002) p.15 'in the sedentary dialects the original interdental fricatives *θ, *ð and *ð̣ merged with the dental stops'; Ennaji et al. (2004) p.2 inventory has no interdentals. ق → /q/ PRESERVED (sedentary/pre-Hilali; /ɡ/ in Bedouin/rural — Watson p.17); Moroccan has both /q/ and /ɡ/ as phonemes (Ennaji p.2). Pharyngeals ħ, ʕ strong. French adstrate: /p/, /v/, /e/, /o/. Berber (Amazigh) substrate underlies the schwa reduction and consonant-cluster phonotactics.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Read (full open-access PDF; printed pages). p.16: in the majority of Maghribi dialects (cf. Heath 1987:20-1 for Moroccan) jīm has no initial occlusive element and is realized as /ž/ [ʒ]. p.15 §2.1.4: sedentary dialects merged the interdental fricatives *θ *ð *ð̣ with the dental stops. p.17 §2.1.9: *q maintained in North African (sedentary) dialects, /ɡ/ in Bedouin. p.13: Maghrebi unstressed-vowel deletion → word-initial clusters (smin vs Cairene simīn).

sources.author: Noamane, A.
sources.title: Consonant Gemination in Moroccan Arabic: A Constraint-Based Analysis
sources.publisher: Journal of Applied Language and Culture Studies 3: 37-68
sources.notes: Read (open-access PDF). p.49 chart 18: MA vocalic inventory limited to three underlying short vowels /i, u, a/ plus epenthetic schwa /ə/, no long vowels. p.45 consonant chart: /ʒ/ voiced alveo-palatal fricative (no /dʒ/); both /q/ (uvular) and /ɡ/ (velar) present.

sources.author: Heath, J.
sources.title: Moroccan Arabic (ch. 10 in Lucas & Manfredi, eds., Arabic and Contact-Induced Change)
sources.notes: Read (open-access PDF). p.216: the koiné reduces all three short vowels to a single short vowel ə (with allophones), original short vowels syncopate in weak metrical positions.

sources.author: Ennaji, M., Makhoukh, A., Es-saiydy, H., Moubtassime, M. & Slaoui, S.
sources.title: A Grammar of Moroccan Arabic
sources.publisher: Publications de la Faculté des Lettres, Fès
sources.notes: Read (open-access PDF). p.2 28-consonant inventory: /ž/ [ʒ] present, no /dʒ/ affricate, no interdentals /θ ð/, both /q/ and /ɡ/. p.7: schwa is epenthetic, appearing between consonants; clusters pp.3-4 (kəlb, smən, smra).

sources.author: Harrell, R.S.
sources.title: A Short Reference Grammar of Moroccan Arabic
sources.notes: Standard descriptive grammar; not obtained to a readable page for this pass (Google Books ToC only).

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Moroccan Arabic.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, §2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVːC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiˈtaːb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVː/CVC) does (muˈdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ˈmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant — a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
